title_en: Madonna Wants to Take You There

paragraphs.en: The pop star launched her new album, “Confessions II,” at a party in London, complete with watery drinks and nostalgic hits. Her co-host for the evening? Grindr.
paragraphs.zh: 这位流行天后在伦敦举办了新专辑《Confessions II》的发布会，现场有清淡鸡尾酒和充满怀旧感的热门歌曲。今晚的联席主持人是？Grindr。

paragraphs.en: The directions were clear: “Go through the legs and up the stairs.” The limbs in question were vast, inflatable, and splayed, maybe fifteen feet high, and bent at the knee, as if a giant were lying on its back. Each leg was shod in a high-heeled silver boot. In between the legs was—no getting around it—a slit, through which all visitors were invited to pass. It was like being born in reverse.
paragraphs.zh: 指示非常清楚：“先从大腿穿过去再上楼。” 那些“腿”硕大、可膨胀、张开着，几乎有十五英尺高，并在膝部弯曲，就像一具巨人仰卧在那儿。每条腿都穿着一只高跟银色靴子。两条腿之间——这是无法回避的现实——有一条缝隙，所有来宾都被邀请从那里经过。那感觉就像反着出生。

paragraphs.en: Such was the reception accorded to anyone lucky enough, and unblushing enough, to attend the launch of Madonna’s latest album, “Confessions II.” The venue was a blockish and forbidding space called Magazine, which squats in a loop of the Thames, in southeast London. The interior was a restful combination of black walls and patinated steel, with polished concrete underfoot. The motto for the occasion was printed on T-shirts available for purchase in a foyer beyond the slit: “Don’t Be a Vibe Kill.” Evidently, Madonna was going industrial—the furthest possible cry from her lady-of-the-manor period in the early years of this century, when she was seen fetchingly clad in rustic tweeds. That vibe is so dead.
paragraphs.zh: 凡是有幸并且不怕尴尬到尴尬程度的人，走进《Confessions II》最新专辑发布会都得到了这样的迎接。举办地是一处叫做“Magazine”的方形又带威慑感的空间，坐落在伦敦东南部泰晤士河的一段弯道上。室内由黑色墙面和古铜色钢板组合而成，脚下是抛光混凝土，给人以安静硬朗的质感。活动口号印在走廊尽头出售的T恤上：“Don’t Be a Vibe Kill。” 显然，麦当娜这次走的是工业路线，这与本世纪初她“女主人”式早期形象形成了最遥远的反差——当时她常穿着带乡村感的粗花呢，穿搭俏皮又优雅。那种氛围已经彻底过气。

paragraphs.en: The evening’s co-hosts were Madonna and Grindr. The resulting throng was described in Rolling Stone, the following day, as “juicily maximalist,” filled with “some of the country’s best dressed people.” These, alas, were hard to spot, the exception being a gentleman whose torso, richly tattooed, was encased in a transparent cuirass. Some guests were granted access to an exclusive balcony on one side of the room, where they were offered a selection of themed cocktails. One drink was named for “Danceteria”—a track from the new album, celebrating the former Manhattan night club—and daringly rimmed in chili powder. Any suggestion that the hot hit of spice barely disguising a watery want of flavor was all too fitting a symbol for this phase in Madonna’s career would have been met with indignation. “Confessions II” has been greeted in some quarters as a late-flowering masterpiece, and, for one night, Magazine was the rightful home of the true believer, not the skeptic.
paragraphs.zh: 当晚的共同主持者是麦当娜和 Grindr。滚石杂志次日称当晚人群“浓郁到极致地追求奢华”，并挤满了“全国最有品味的人”。可惜这些人不易分辨，除了一位绅士外——他的躯干上密密麻麻刺着纹身，包裹在一件透明胸甲里。部分来宾被允许进入房间一侧的专属阳台区，在那里可以品尝一系列主题鸡尾酒。其中一款以《Danceteria》命名，这是新专辑里的同名曲目，向曼哈顿旧日夜总会致敬，杯沿则大胆裹了辣椒粉。若有人暗示，辛辣的外表几乎掩盖了风味淡薄的“主旋律”，这正好象征了麦当娜职业生涯这一阶段，这样的评论会立即引起强烈反感。对某些圈层而言，《Confessions II》被视为绽放较晚却称得上杰作的一张专辑，而在那个夜晚，“Magazine”真正属于坚定追随者，而不是怀疑者。

paragraphs.en: The church of Madonna has many denominations, and, as a blunt rule, the stuff you like depends on the age you are—or, at any rate, the age that your clubbing self would like to be. On the Tube to North Greenwich, the stop nearest the venue, Louis Byrne, a hair stylist and the founder of a company called I Can I Am and I Will, said, “My mum was very envious that I was coming tonight”—his mother being three years older than Madonna and, in Byrne’s words, “definitely a ‘Blond Ambition’ girl.” He himself cherishes “my first memory of ‘Vogue’ on the caravan-park-disco dance floor as a young boy.” On Magazine’s balcony, Timothy Phillips, a historian of the Cold War, admitted to a preference for “Confessions on a Dance Floor,” from 2005, and for its opening number, “Hung Up,” which chugged its way to the summit of the charts in more than forty countries. Would Madonna be scaling that peak at the party?
paragraphs.zh: 麦当娜有许多“教派”，而且一个粗略的规则是，你喜欢哪一部分，通常取决于你现在的年龄——或者更准确地说，取决于你的“夜店自我”想要处在的年龄。搭乘通往诺斯格林威治的地铁（离会场最近的站点）时，发型师、名为“I Can I Am and I Will”公司的创始人路易斯·拜恩说：“我妈妈非常羡慕我今晚会来。”他妈妈比麦当娜年长三岁，而且在拜恩的话里，她“绝对是个‘Blond Ambition’女孩”。他自己珍藏着“我第一次听到《Vogue》是在童年在露营地舞池上的记忆”。在《杂志》阳台上，冷战历史学家蒂莫西·菲利普斯承认自己偏爱2005年的《Confessions on a Dance Floor》以及它的开场曲《Hung Up》，后者在四十多个国家登上了排行榜榜首。麦当娜今晚会在派对上重现那种巅峰状态吗？

paragraphs.en: Yes, but it was a hell of a wait. Having been urged to arrive between nine-thirty and ten, guests were placed in the care of d.j.s until midnight, at which point Stuart Price, the producer of both “Confessions” albums, took to the decks. The mob, hitherto restless, surged to life, roaring the chorus of Madonna’s 2000 song “Music.” (The literary equivalent would be a publisher reading out bits of old novels at an author’s book launch.) At last, Madonna materialized. She wore shades and a loosely tied pink coat, which, when removed, revealed a shiny pink top and long pink gloves; it was as if the former queen of bubblegum pop were made of actual gum. Standing next to Price, she sang along to herself, a little, and issued moral commands. “Be whoever you want to be!” she cried, pointing a finger at the mob, like a much loved teacher advising her third-grade pupils on Halloween costumes.
paragraphs.zh: 是的，但等待过程确实很煎熬。来宾被要求在晚上一点钟到十点之间入场，却被DJ们安置到午夜才开始，随后《Confessions》两张专辑的制作人斯图尔特·普赖斯登上了混音台。此前躁动不安的人群终于爆发出来，齐声喊起麦当娜2000年的歌曲《Music》的副歌。（在文学界，大概相当于一位主编在作家新书发布会上读出旧小说的零散片段。）终于，麦当娜现身了。她戴着墨镜，外披一件系得松散的粉色大衣，脱下后露出一件闪亮的粉色上衣和长粉色手套；那一刻仿佛曾经的泡泡糖流行乐皇后真是由糖胶做成的一样。她站在普赖斯身边，轻轻地哼唱着自己刚才唱过的旋律，并发出像命令般的呼吁。“成为你想成为的人吧！”她指着人群喊道，像一位深受喜爱的老师在万圣节前给三年级学生指点服装。

paragraphs.en: The shindig was nicely timed to end after the last train from North Greenwich had departed. The crowd was reborn through the spread legs and stranded at the mercy of small-hours London. Buses were welcomed like lifeboats. Phillips, asked to sum up Madonna’s performance, sought the ideal comparison, and found it. “It was like Serena,” he said—Serena Williams, that is, who, two days earlier, had come to London and, in the aftermath of her prime, played and lost a singles match at Wimbledon. A valiant defeat, in other words, and far from ignoble. Other experts held court. “Did we all think Madonna was a letdown?” one of them, in a rainbow vest, inquired. Back came the tolerant reply: “Come on, she is sixty-seven.” Yet who would not bet on further reincarnations? Away the revellers wandered, into the groove of the night. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 这场聚会在诺斯格林威治最后一班列车已经离站后，恰到好处地结束。人群在夜深人静、伦敦清冷的城市里被困的命运中重新散开。公交车被当作救生艇般欢迎。菲利普斯被请来总结麦当娜的表演，想了个“理想”的比喻，也找到了答案。“这就像赛琳娜。”他指的是塞丽娜·威廉姆斯——这位两天前刚到伦敦的她，在巅峰之后的阶段，于温网打完了一场单打却输掉了比赛。也就是说，这是场勇敢的输球，并且绝非可耻的失败。其他点评者接着发言。“我们都觉得麦当娜令人大失所望吗？”一位穿着彩虹背心的人问道。随即传来宽容的回应：“得了吧，她已经67岁了。”然而，谁能不押注她还会继续再度“重生”呢？随后，欢聚者们离开，沉入夜色的节奏里。♦
